facade_colour: buff/cream brick with green trim and white accents

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair with 76 Bellevue Avenue, the property retains many of its original building features including its shared roofline with central gable, first-storey bay window, segmentally arched openings, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. The property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: buff/cream brick with green painted trim and white window surrounds
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Generally good condition. Some areas of stucco/paint over original brick at ground level. Modern balcony addition at second floor with metal railing. Green-painted eavestroughs and downspouts.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor has two windows flanking entry, appearing to be replacement casement windows. Second floor has two windows with segmental arched heads visible in brickwork. White-painted frames.
photo_observations.door_details: Central entrance with modern door, small covered porch/canopy above entry
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple eave line with green-painted fascia
photo_observations.porch_details: Small covered entry porch with flat canopy, 3 steps up from grade
photo_observations.decorative_details: Segmental brick arches over second-floor windows. Buff brick is a warm cream colour. Green trim provides accent colour.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Gable roof, shared with 76 Bellevue Ave
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Modern balcony added at second floor with metal railings. Replacement windows. Ground floor appears modified with stucco/paint covering. Green-painted trim is likely not original.
photo_observations.notes: 2-storey semi-detached Victorian house paired with 76 Bellevue Ave, sharing central gable roofline. Buff/cream brick facade rather than red brick. HCD mentions bay window at first storey but none visible from this angle - may be on side or removed. Segmental arched window openings visible at second storey consistent with HCD description.

notes: 2-storey semi-detached Victorian. Buff/cream brick, green trim, shared central gable with 76 Bellevue Ave. Modern balcony addition at second floor.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include original windows.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair with 76 Bellevue Avenue, the property retains many of its original building features including its shared roofline with central gable, first-storey bay window, segmentally arched openings, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached two-storey house-form in buff/cream brick with green trim and white accents with a gable roof. Features include simple bargeboards. Modern balcony added at second floor with metal railings. Replacement windows. Ground floor appears modified with stucco/paint covering. Green-painted trim is likely not original. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair with 76 Bellevue Avenue, the property retains many of its original building features including its shared roofline with central gable, first-storey bay window, segmentally arched openings, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
